GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "The love story worth drinking to this weekend", "We'll cheers to that!", "Valentine's Day is the one day of the year you can be unabashedly in love – that much is a fact. Whether you're celebrating your one-of-a-kind partner or getting together with friends to honour your galentines, there's something beautiful about celebrating all things love just for one day. So, what better time than now for a love story? This year, we're honouring all things love by sharing the story of Perrier-Jouet. French Champagne makers from the early 1800s, Pierre-Nicolas Perrier married Rose-Adelaide in 1811, launching a love story which would culminate in one of France's most distinctive champagne houses. Rose coloured glasses, anyone? It was a fate destined to be, with the year of their marriage immortalised in history as the famed Year Of The Comet, canonised by Leo Tolstoy in his seminal novel War and Peace. A famed astronomical event, the comet shot over the earth, leaving a trail of shimmering dust in the sky visible for a whole 260 days. That same year, Perrier-Jouet's vineyard got its first big break. In September, when the comet was closest to the sun, the region had unseasonably warm weather, leading to a superb year for wine. Subsequently, the newlyweds saw their inaugural cuvee sell out fast, giving them the financial backing they needed to launch the business. In a sweet nod to the fate of the stars, they named the drop Wine of the Comet. Rose-Adelaide was no doubt the brains behind the business, running client relationships, keeping the accounts and travelling alone to Paris to do business when required – quite a bold move for the time. Combined with Pierre-Nicolas' sophisticated palette and winemaking skills, the Perrier-Jouet house was soon served in some of the most prestigious royal courts across Europe. Since, the Perrier-Jouet house has become one of the most renowned in the world. Based out of Epernay, north-east of Paris, the house is famed for its classic takes on Champagne, particularly its signature rose, Perrier-Jouet Blason Rose – the perfect drop for a Valentine's celebration. A gourmet cuvee harmonizing Perrier-Jouet's signature floral style with luscious fruity notes, the Champagne tastes as good as it looks, with a design-led bottle perfect for gifting. Inspired by Maison Perrier-Jouet's Blason de France prestige cuvees, the Blason Rose is an ultra refined gift ideal for the person you love most. So, why not toast to Pierre-Nicolas Perrier and Rose-Adelaide Jouet this Valentine's Day? Here's to love! Visit:perrier-jouet.com and shop Perrier-Jouet's Blason Rose now at Vintage Cellars."
